purpose: Particle-driven coral/barnacle growth on a mesh surface. Mesh Emitter spawns particles on the head bust; Field Condition gates by Random Field overlap; survivors voxelize via Volume Builder + Volume Mesher producing polygonal coral protrusions seamlessly fused with the head.
visual_result: Head bust with vertex-color heat map (blue → green → yellow) marking field intensity, plus bumpy yellow/green coral clusters growing from the surface where particles survived the field gate. Ear, nose, top-of-head all covered with coral protrusions.
input_geometry: Generic Head Bust (5100, polygon mesh) wrapped in Subdivision Surface, preprocessed by a Geometry Axis Nodes Modifier.

hosts.role: preprocesses input mesh axis alignment via bounding-box of selected points
Y-axis range mask on volume output (post-process). MINIMAL Nodes Modifier — only pointsmodifier@ + framework.

doc_level_objects.name: Random Field
doc_level_objects.role: drives Field Condition particle gating + per-vertex heat map on head

om_classic_generators.name: Mesh Emitter
om_classic_generators.role: emits particles from head bust surface

om_classic_generators.name: Field Condition
om_classic_generators.role: particle modifier — tests overlap with Random Field

om_classic_generators.name: Kill
om_classic_generators.role: particle modifier — kills failed Field Condition particles

om_classic_generators.name: Switch Group
om_classic_generators.role: particle modifier — transfers survivors to Particle Group Grow

om_classic_generators.name: Particle Group Grow
om_classic_generators.role: destination group — fed into Volume Builder

om_classic_generators.name: Connect
om_classic_generators.role: OM-level Connect generator — combines Volume Mesher + Mask Y outputs

om_classic_generators.name: Volume Builder
om_classic_generators.role: unions Particle Group Grow + Subdivision Surface into SDF

om_classic_generators.name: Subdivision Surface
om_classic_generators.role: smooths Generic Head Bust before voxelization

annotation_storage_pattern: No OM-tag annotations; no scaffold@ section labels in Nodes Modifier graphs (their tiny size doesn't need them).

am_sliders_geometry_axis.name: Relative Position
am_sliders_geometry_axis.controls: axis offset relative to bounding box

am_exposure_pattern_note: Geometry Axis uses floatingio wrappers (4 params). Mask Y has zero AM exposure — entirely self-contained. Both extremes possible in the Nodes Modifier family.

key_connections.purpose: Emit new particles into the initial group.
Field overlap drives particle pass/fail test.
Particles failing Field Condition get killed.
Particles passing get switched into Particle Group Grow.
Surviving particles feed into the Volume Builder for SDF unioning.
Subdivided head fed into the SAME Volume Builder, unioned with particles.
Smoothed polygonal output goes into the OM-level Connect.
Final post-process Y-range clip via the 5-node Nodes Modifier.
Pre-process axis re-alignment of the input mesh.

output_routing.final_node: Connect (OM-level) wrapping the whole stack; Mask Y Nodes Modifier as final post-process
output_routing.via: Mask Y output → Connect → visible polygon mesh in OM
output_routing.host_visibility_path: Connect renders as classic mesh; the SDF math + Nodes Modifier deformations all baked into its child caches.
output_routing.notes: Distinct from previous scenes — output isn't a single Nodes host's emit; it's a CHAIN of OM generators with embedded Nodes Modifiers.

interesting_types: Particle Group membership as selection mechanism (alternative to Scene Nodes setselection@)
Volume Builder unioning two stream types (particles + mesh) into one SDF
5-node Nodes Modifier (just pointsmodifier@ + framework) — proves tiny shippable units exist
20-node Nodes Modifier with bounding-box-of-selected-points for axis re-alignment
Mesh Emitter + Particle Group + Field Condition + Kill + Switch Group = full particle filter chain

core_idea_nodes.name: Field Condition + Kill + Switch Group (particle modifier chain)
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Field-gated particle filtering. Field Condition tests overlap; Kill removes failures; Switch Group transfers survivors. The canonical particle-side equivalent of Scene Nodes' selection-by-field workflow.

core_idea_nodes.name: Volume Builder (1039859)
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Unions BOTH particle stream AND mesh stream into one SDF. Demonstrates that volume builders are stream-type-agnostic — feed it anything point-like, get a fused SDF.

core_idea_nodes.name: Mask Y (5-node Nodes Modifier)
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Proves a useful Nodes Modifier can be just pointsmodifier@ + framework. No floatingio, no multi-node logic — single-purpose deformer in 5 nodes total.

core_idea_nodes.name: Geometry Axis (20-node Nodes Modifier)
core_idea_nodes.why_it_matters: Bounding-box-of-selected-points → re-aligned axis system. Useful preprocessing for any scene needing consistent up/forward direction.

scaffolding_nodes: context_externaltimeinput / context_notime / framework groups in both Nodes Modifiers
what_is_clever: Two Nodes Modifiers at different pipeline stages — Geometry Axis preprocesses input, Mask Y postprocesses output. Demonstrates Nodes Modifier composition.
Particle Group membership as selection mechanism — equivalent to setselection@ but in particle-space. Field Condition + Kill + Switch Group is the canonical particle filter chain.
5-node Nodes Modifier (Mask Y) proves tiny shippable units exist. Single-purpose deformers don't need elaborate AM exposure.
Volume Builder unions multiple stream types (particles + mesh) into one fused SDF — output looks seamless.
Vertex-color heat map on the head (visible at f0) shows the Random Field intensity directly on the input mesh BEFORE any particle simulation runs.

gotchas.issue: Particle scenes timeout when scrubbed via SetTime per-frame. Each call triggers full particle recompute → wedges main thread.
gotchas.evidence: Sequential 0..50 stepping plus screenshots → 30s execute_python timeout at f50. Confirmed by Spenser's correction: 'with something like particles you may want to PLAY rather than scrub'.
gotchas.workaround: For particle/dynamics scenes: trust mid-state captures, use playback, or pre-cook the simulation. Detect via Mesh Emitter / Particle Group / Field Condition presence.

gotchas.issue: Nodes Modifier (180420400) hosts ALSO use Neutron in this scene — confirms gotcha #56 covers the whole 180420400/500/600/700 family.
gotchas.evidence: Both Geometry Axis and Mask Y returned net.maxon.neutron.nodespace from GetAllNimbusRefs().
gotchas.workaround: Always probe GetAllNimbusRefs() for any 1804204XX host.

gotchas.issue: C4D 2026's new particle system (Mesh Emitter, Particle Group, Field Condition, etc.) doesn't surface parameters cleanly via host.GetDescription() walk.
gotchas.evidence: Description walk returned no usable parameter names for any of the 6 particle objects.
gotchas.workaround: Use param-specific descid lookups (or wait for cinema4d-mcp to expose particle-aware introspection helpers).

rebuild_recipe: 2. Inside Connect: Volume Mesher (1039861) + Mask Y Nodes Modifier (180420400, 5-node minimal: pointsmodifier@).
3. Inside Volume Mesher: Volume Builder (1039859) + Smoothing (1024529).
4. Inside Volume Builder: Particle Group Grow (1060887) + Subdivision Surface wrapping Generic Head Bust + Geometry Axis Nodes Modifier (180420400, 20-node).
5. As siblings of Connect: Mesh Emitter (1062577), Particle Group init (1060887) with Field Condition (1062533) → Kill (1061199), Switch Group (1062045).
7. Wire Field Condition's field reference to Random Field.
8. Wire Switch Group's destination to Particle Group Grow.
9. Set Mesh Emitter's Source to the head mesh.
10. Tune: Field Condition.Tolerance, Volume Builder.Voxel Size, Mask Y range, Mesh Emitter.Rate.

minimal_reproducible_subgraph.name: particle_to_volume_growth
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.purpose: Spawn particles on a mesh surface, gate by field overlap, voxelize survivors + input mesh together → polygonal coral-style growth.
nodes: Volume Mesher → Volume Builder → Particle Group Grow + Subdivision Surface > input mesh > Geometry Axis Nodes Modifier
Particle Group init > Field Condition > Kill, Switch Group (sibling chain)
(generic input mesh — bust, hull, rock, etc.)
exposed_params: Field Condition.Tolerance — overlap threshold % gating particles
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.value_proposition: The canonical 'particles seed volumetric growth on a host mesh' archetype. Generalizes to: barnacles on hulls, moss on rocks, mushrooms on logs, alien protrusions on characters, crystal clusters on terrain.
secondary_recipes: R18_field_gated_particle_filter — Mesh Emitter + Particle Group + Field Condition + Kill + Switch Group (5-object particle filter chain)
R19_minimal_nodes_modifier — 5-node pointsmodifier-only deformer (Mask Y as pattern)
R20_axis_realignment_modifier — 20-node Geometry Axis pattern for bounding-box pivot re-alignment
blockers_to_recipe_implementation: C4D 2026 particle system (Mesh Emitter, Particle Groups, particle modifiers) not yet exposed via cinema4d-mcp
Particle-aware viewport_screenshot needed (current scrub-pattern times out)
Recipe composition system needed — combine R17 from sub-recipes R18+R19+R20

lessons_for_cinema4d_mcp: Plugin 180420400 (Nodes Modifier) ALSO uses Neutron in modern scenes. Gotcha #56 covers the whole 180420400/500/600/700 family.
Tiny Nodes Modifiers are shippable (5-node Mask Y). Recipe library should include minimal templates for single-purpose deformers.
Particle-Group-as-Selection is a documentable pattern alongside Scene Nodes setselection@.
viewport_screenshot needs a particle-aware variant. Detect via Mesh Emitter/Particle Group/Field Condition presence; trust mid-state or use playback.
Recipe composition is essential for multi-subsystem scenes — R17 = R18 + R19 + R20 + glue, not 50 individual operations.

frames: frames/default_f025.png (full coral coverage, all bumps grown)
artifacts.captures_skipped: f50, f100 timed out due to particle compute exceeding 30s; methodology note added
